calendar.matters:
PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joy Egu (Petitioner) in propria persona.

The Court inquired whether Ms. Egu had tried to give notice to the children of her deceased husband. Ms. Egu stated that she had attempted to contact his first son, she left voicemail messages, sent him a text message, that she finally notified him two weeks ago, and that she had told him that she was going to court to establish the marriage between she and his Father. In addition, she stated that there are three other children of her deceased husband: Idurii Mufa, Ngozi Okoronkwo, and Nkemdirim Nkwokeji that she did not contact because they do not like her, and that she has two children. The Court inquired as to what constituted a legal marriage in Nigeria. Ms. Egu stated that there is a traditional marriage where the man goes to the woman's family and brings money/ a dowry; it is a big occasion, and that occurred in 1985. In addition, she stated that then there is a Court/Church marriage, that when she came to join him in the United States they had a Court marriage, and that it was done for another legal protection. Ms. Egu stated that her deceased husband was married to his first Wife in approxiamately 1963 and it ended in 1978, that it officially ended; there was a divorce in 1985, and she provided the Court with a copy of the divorce certificate which was shown in open court. Ms. Egu addressed the Co-Respondent issue as set forth on the record. Additional discussions were held. The Court granted the Petition to Establish Fact of Marriage (order signed).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
